query: What are the major phases, decision points and associated fees, if any, in the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process?
final_answer:
# NYISO Generation Interconnection Cluster Study Process: Major Phases, Decision Points, and Fees

The NYISO's Cluster Study Process, established under **OATT Attachment HH** (effective May 2, 2024) in compliance with FERC Order No. 2023, replaced the former serial study process (feasibility study, system impact study, and facilities study) with a single, two-phase clustered approach. The total process timeline is approximately **590 calendar days (~1.6 years)**. Below is a comprehensive breakdown of each phase, decision point, and associated financial commitment.

**Sources:** NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.2 ([nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%252040.2.pdf)); NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview, Dec. 14, 2023 OC Meeting ([nyiso.com/documents/20142/41716870/07b](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/41716870/07b_NYISO%20Cluster%20Study%20Process%20Overview%20Figure_OC_30231214.pdf)); NYISO Standard Interconnection Procedures FAQ ([nyiso.com/documents/20142/49802416](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/49802416/NYISOs-Standard-Interconnection-Procedures-FAQ.pdf)); NYISO Transition Cluster Study Technical Bulletin ([nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf)); NYISO Notice of Transition Cluster Study Application Window, July 1, 2024 ([nyiso.com/documents/20142/1396587](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1396587/Notice-of-the-Transition-Cluster-Study-Application-Window.pdf)); NYISO IITF Presentation Nov. 2, 2023 ([nyiso.com/documents/20142/40995296](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/40995296/Order%20No.%202023%20Compliance%20Plan_IITF_20231102_final%20version.pdf)).

---

## Phase 0: Pre-Application Process (Optional, Rolling)

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Description** | A voluntary, rolling process available before the Application Window opens. Prospective Interconnection Customers can review the publicly posted **Heatmap** (interactive visual of estimated incremental injection capacity) and/or request a **Pre-Application Report** from the Connecting Transmission Owner. |
| **Fee** | No specific fee identified for the Heatmap. A Pre-Application Report involves a scoping meeting with the Connecting Transmission Owner, who must complete the report within **25 business days**. |
| **Purpose** | Provides early information on potential points of interconnection, grid constraints, and planned upgrades to help developers make informed siting decisions before committing to the formal process. |

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.4; NYISO FAQ §4–5; NYISO blog post Aug. 1, 2024 ([nyiso.com/-/enhancing-the-interconnection-process](https://www.nyiso.com/-/enhancing-the-interconnection-process-the-nyisos-commitment-to-improving-the-integration-of-new-generation-projects-onto-the-grid)).*

---

## Phase 1: Application Window

| Element | Standard Cluster | Transition Cluster |
|---|---|---|
| **Duration** | 45 Calendar Days | 75 Calendar Days |

### What Happens
- The NYISO opens the Application Window on the Cluster Study Process Start Date.
- Interconnection Customers submit an **Interconnection Request** (or **CRIS-Only Request**) along with all required technical data, conceptual one-line diagrams, workable project models, site control demonstrations, and (for IBRs >20 MW) NYSRC Reliability Rule B.5 attestations.
- NYISO validates submissions on a rolling basis within **10 business days** (15 business days for the Transition Cluster).
- Deficiencies must be cured within **10 business days** (15 for Transition) or by the end of the Application Window.

### Fees & Deposits at Entry

| Fee/Deposit | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Application Fee** (non-refundable, cash only) | **$10,000** for Interconnection Requests; **$5,000** for CRIS-Only Requests | 75% allocated to NYISO, 25% to Connecting TO/Affected TOs |
| **Study Deposit (SD)** (cash, Letter of Credit, or surety bond) | **< 80 MW:** $100,000; **≥ 80 MW and < 200 MW:** $150,000; **≥ 200 MW:** $250,000; **CRIS-Only:** $50,000 | Held for the duration of the process; subject to withdrawal penalties; refunded in full if the project accepts its cost allocation and posts security at the end of Phase 2. NYISO invoices actual study costs monthly and draws on the deposit only if the customer fails to pay. |

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.5; NYISO Notice of Transition Cluster Study Application Window (July 1, 2024); NYISO IITF Nov. 2, 2023 presentation.*

---

## Phase 2: Customer Engagement Window

| Element | Standard Cluster | Transition Cluster |
|---|---|---|
| **Duration** | 70 Calendar Days | 90 Calendar Days |

### What Happens
1. **Cluster Study Project List Published** — Within 10 business days of the Application Window closing, NYISO publishes the list of all validated Cluster Study Projects in the cluster.
2. **Physical Infeasibility Screening** — The Connecting Transmission Owner conducts a screening to determine whether any proposed interconnections are physically infeasible (i.e., no technically viable means to connect at the proposed POI). Projects deemed physically infeasible **cannot proceed** and are withdrawn.
3. **Scoping Meeting** — NYISO hosts a group scoping meeting for the entire cluster.
4. **Limited POI Change** — Interconnection Customers may request a limited change to their Point of Interconnection within 5 business days of the cluster member list posting.

### Decision Point: No formal go/no-go decision during this window, but the Physical Infeasibility Screen is a critical gate.

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.7; NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure.*

---

## Phase 3: Phase 1 Entry Decision Period

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Duration** | **5 Business Days** |
| **Decision** | The Interconnection Customer must elect to either: **(a) Proceed** to the Phase 1 Study by posting **Readiness Deposit 1 (RD1)**, or **(b) Withdraw** from the Queue. |

### Financial Commitment to Proceed

| Deposit | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Readiness Deposit 1 (RD1)** | **$4,000/MW** of the proposed facility |

### Withdrawal Penalty 1 (WP1)

| Condition | Penalty |
|---|---|
| Standard withdrawal | **25% of Study Deposit (SD)** |
| If interconnection is physically infeasible | **$5,000** flat |
| If project withdraws within 5 business days of cluster member list posting | **$0** (penalty-free) |

*Source: NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure (Dec. 14, 2023); NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.7.5–40.7.6.*

---

## Phase 4: Phase 1 Study (Cluster Study Phase 1)

| Element | Standard Cluster | Transition Cluster |
|---|---|---|
| **Duration** | 190 Calendar Days | 190 Calendar Days |

### What Happens
- The NYISO finalizes the **Existing System Representation** (the base case power flow model).
- The **Connecting Transmission Owner(s)** and **Affected Transmission Owner(s)** perform the Phase 1 Study to identify:
  - **Connecting Transmission Owner Attachment Facilities (CTOAFs)**
  - **Local System Upgrade Facilities (Local SUFs)**
  - **Distribution Upgrades**
- Cost estimates and preliminary construction schedules are provided for each identified facility.
- Pre-work is performed to prepare for Phase 2 MIS/DIS analyses.
- The Phase 1 Study concludes with the **Operating Committee's approval of the Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report**.

### Fees
- No additional deposit is required at the start of Phase 1 (RD1 was posted at the Phase 1 Entry Decision Period).
- Actual study costs continue to be invoiced monthly.

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.10; NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure.*

---

## Phase 5: Phase 2 Entry Decision Period

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Duration** | **10 Business Days** |
| **Decision** | The Interconnection Customer must elect to either: **(a) Proceed** to the Phase 2 Study by posting **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)**, or **(b) Withdraw** from the Queue. |

### Financial Commitment to Proceed

| Deposit | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)** | The **greater of**: (i) RD1 ($4,000/MW), and (ii) **20% of the cost estimate** determined in Phase 1 for Local SUFs and CTOAFs |

> *Note: RD1 and RD2 are cumulative, not additive — RD2 replaces/supersedes RD1 at the higher amount.*

### Withdrawal Penalty 2 (WP2)

| Condition | Penalty |
|---|---|
| Standard withdrawal | **50% of SD + 10% of RD1** |
| If interconnection is physically infeasible | **25% of SD** only |

*Source: NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure; NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.10.8–40.10.9; NYISO IITF Nov. 2, 2023 presentation.*

---

## Phase 6: Phase 2 Study (Cluster Study Phase 2)

| Element | Standard Cluster | Transition Cluster |
|---|---|---|
| **Duration** | 270 Calendar Days | 270 Calendar Days |

### What Happens
- The NYISO develops the **Cluster Baseline Assessment** and **Cluster Project Assessment** base cases.
- The NYISO performs:
  - **Minimum Interconnection Standard (MIS)** assessments — identifying **System Upgrade Facilities (SUFs)** and **Distribution Upgrades** required for reliable interconnection of ERIS-requesting projects.
  - **Cluster Study Deliverability Study (DIS)** — for projects requesting **Capacity Resource Interconnection Service (CRIS)**, identifying required **System Deliverability Upgrades (SDUs)**.
- The Connecting TO/Affected TO updates cost estimates and construction schedules for all facilities identified in both Phase 1 and Phase 2.
- The Phase 2 Study concludes with the **Operating Committee's approval of the Cluster Study Report**.

### Fees
- No additional deposit at the start of Phase 2 (RD2 was posted at the Phase 2 Entry Decision Period).
- Actual study costs continue to be invoiced monthly.

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.11–40.13; NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure.*

---

## Phase 7: Final Decision Period (Iterative)

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Duration** | Iterative (multiple rounds) |
| **Decision** | Each Interconnection Customer elects, through iterative decision rounds, whether to **accept its Project Cost Allocation** and **pay cash or post Security** for all identified upgrades (CTOAFs, Distribution Upgrades, SUFs, and/or SDUs), or to **withdraw**. |

### Financial Commitment to Proceed

| Requirement | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Security** | **100% of the cost estimate** of all SUFs, SDUs, and CTOAFs identified in Phase 1 and Phase 2 |

### Withdrawal Penalty 3 (WP3)

| Condition | Penalty |
|---|---|
| Standard withdrawal | **100% of SD + 20% of RD2** |
| If interconnection is physically infeasible | **50% of SD** only |
| If total cost estimate increased >50% between Phase 1 and Phase 2 (excluding SDU costs) | **100% of SD** only (no RD2 penalty) |

### Security Forfeiture
- If a project accepts its cost allocation, posts security, and **later withdraws**, it may forfeit **up to 100% of its Security** if other projects in the cluster are relying on the upgrades identified for that project.

### Use of Withdrawal Penalty Funds
- Collected withdrawal penalties are first used to **offset study costs** of remaining projects in the cluster that accepted their cost allocations and posted security.
- Any excess penalty funds beyond actual study costs are used to **offset Rate Schedule 1 costs** (NYISO administrative costs).

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.15; NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure; NYISO IITF Nov. 2, 2023 presentation.*

---

## Phase 8: Additional SDU Study (If Applicable)

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Conducted when projects withdraw during the Final Decision Period, potentially freeing up capacity or changing the set of required SDUs for remaining projects. |
| **Decision Period** | An **Additional SDU Study Decision Period** follows, in which remaining customers make final elections. |

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.14; NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure.*

---

## Phase 9: Negotiation of Agreements & Construction

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Agreements** | Projects that accept their cost allocations and post security proceed to negotiate a **Standard Interconnection Agreement (IA)** and any required **Standard Upgrade Construction Agreement(s)** or **Standard Multiparty Upgrade Construction Agreement(s)**. |
| **Engineering & Procurement** | An optional **Engineering & Procurement Agreement** may be entered into earlier to accelerate procurement of long-lead items. |
| **Construction** | Construction of CTOAFs, SUFs, and SDUs proceeds per the agreed schedules. |

*Source: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.19–40.21.*

---

## Summary Table: All Fees, Deposits, and Penalties

| Financial Instrument | Amount | When Required |
|---|---|---|
| **Application Fee** | $10,000 ($5,000 CRIS-Only) | Application Window (non-refundable) |
| **Study Deposit (SD)** | $100K / $150K / $250K by size; $50K CRIS-Only | Application Window |
| **Readiness Deposit 1 (RD1)** | $4,000/MW | Phase 1 Entry Decision Period |
| **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)** | Greater of RD1 or 20% of Phase 1 local upgrade cost estimate | Phase 2 Entry Decision Period |
| **Security** | 100% of all upgrade cost estimates (SUFs + SDUs + CTOAFs) | Final Decision Period |

| Withdrawal Penalty | Amount |
|---|---|
| **WP1** (at/before Phase 1 entry) | 25% of SD (or $5,000 if physically infeasible; $0 if within 5 BD of list posting) |
| **WP2** (at/before Phase 2 entry) | 50% of SD + 10% of RD1 (or 25% of SD if physically infeasible) |
| **WP3** (at/before Final Decision) | 100% of SD + 20% of RD2 (reduced if physically infeasible or >50% cost increase) |
| **Security Forfeiture** (post-acceptance withdrawal) | Up to 100% of Security if other projects rely on the upgrades |

---

## Visual Timeline Summary

```
Pre-Application ──► Application Window ──► Customer Engagement ──► Phase 1 Entry ──► Phase 1 Study ──► Phase 2 Entry ──► Phase 2 Study ──► Final Decision ──► IA Negotiation
  (Optional)          (45/75 days)          Window (70/90 days)     Decision (5 BD)    (190 days)      Decision (10 BD)   (270 days)      (Iterative)
                                                                                      
                      App Fee + SD          Phys. Infeasibility     Post RD1           Local upgrades   Post RD2           SUFs/SDUs       Post Security
                                              Screen + Scoping      or Withdraw        + CTOAFs         or Withdraw        + Deliverability  or Withdraw
                                              Meeting               (WP1)              (OC approval)    (WP2)              (OC approval)     (WP3)
```

*Total Timeline: ~590 Calendar Days (1.6 years)*

steps.reasoning: The user is asking about the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process - its major phases, decision points, and associated fees. This is a specific regulatory/process question about New York Independent System Operator's interconnection procedures. Let me search for this information.

tool_input.query: NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process phases decision points fees
NYISO interconnection process cluster study SRD OSD fees milestones 2024

NYISO invoices actual study costs monthly and draws on the deposit only if the customer fails to pay. |

| Element | Standard Cluster | Transition Cluster |

---

## Phase 5: Phase 2 Entry Decision Period

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Duration** | **10 Business Days** |
| **Decision** | The Interconnection Customer must elect to either: **(a) Proceed** to the Phase 2 Study by posting **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)**, or **(b) Withdraw** from the Queue. |

### Financial Commitment to Proceed

| Deposit | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)** | The **greater of**: (i) RD1 ($4,000/MW), and (ii) **20% of the cost estimate** determined in Phase 1 for Local SUFs and CTOAFs |

| Condition | Penalty |
|---|---|

| Element | Standard Cluster | Transition Cluster |
|---|---|---|
| **Duration** | 270 Calendar Days | 270 Calendar Days |

### What Happens

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Duration** | Iterative (multiple rounds) |
| **Decision** | Each Interconnection Customer elects, through iterative decision rounds, whether to **accept its Project Cost Allocation** and **pay cash or post Security** for all identified upgrades (CTOAFs, Distribution Upgrades, SUFs, and/or SDUs), or to **withdraw**. |

| Requirement | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Security** | **100% of the cost estimate** of all SUFs, SDUs, and CTOAFs identified in Phase 1 and Phase 2 |

| Condition | Penalty |
|---|---|

| Financial Instrument | Amount | When Required |

|---|---|

---

## Visual Timeline Summary
